commands: ask lyle about the mile road

events.summary: Old Lyle: “Newcomer. You've the look of someone after the core. Everyone is, lately. Sit. Ask. Knowing's cheaper here than at the Survey, and worth about what you pay.”

intent.raw: ask lyle about the mile road

events.summary: Old Lyle: “The road? Aye — don't look back on her. Look back and she puts the whole walk behind you again, twice over. Folk have died walking home down a road that kept making more of itself.”
Old Lyle: “The bottoms eat gold, they say — keep your coin deep and you'll be fine.”
Eun of the Survey: “The Survey trades in knowing. You bring me an observation or a coin, I give you a law you can stake your life on. That is the whole of the arrangement.”
The next milepost reads the same numeral as the last one — and the one before that. The road is not counting honestly. The distance behind you is not what it was.
You stop, and turn, and look back the way you came.
You glance back — and the road behind you yawns suddenly twice as long, the waystation dwindled to a far grey smudge. Something in you slips a notch out of true. (You are Unsettled.)
Something in you slips a notch out of true. (Unsettled.)
The light goes thin and orange. Dusk settles over the Hush.

A salvager sits against a milepost, long dead, facing back the way she came. The bootprints around her loop and double and loop again, a thousand steps that never got her home. She kept looking back. The road kept giving her more road to look at. She never reached the end of it.
You are certain now. The Mile Road: On the Mile Road, to look back is to double the distance behind you.
Rust blooms across every worked rivet and blade like frost across a window — fast enough to watch it spread. Worked iron is rotting here, and quickly. Something in the dark un-makes it.
A low hum comes up through the soles of your boots — and you feel it answer, faintly, in the iron you carry. The hum is in the metal. It is calling your worked iron back to the ground it came from.
You are certain now. The Greywater: In the Greywater after dark, worked metal slumps back toward ore. Iron tools and blades fail.
The last of the light fails. It is night now, and the Zone wakes.
